creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
Unluckily I need to correct my stance a little.

I took for granted what you said that it was only a meson thing to not have a specific CDDB toggle, but looking at upstream code things are not that easy.

CDDB support in the source code is embedded in CDDA support, as in embedded in the source code and UI design (there is no alternative UI without the CDDB fields for the CDDA configuration). [1]

There is no define or other way to exclude compilation of CDDB parts, so to remove CDDB support I'd have to sacrifice CDDA support (which I do not use and have not been using for a while, but it could be useful).

I don't have the time resources needed for the development effort required in the upstream project to separate support.

So I need to paddle back, what would be an acceptable resolution from me?

Following ones are not mutually exclusive:

- Disable CDDA support by default
- Change CDDA default configuration to disable CDDB by default/change default server
- add pkg-install note advising that CDDB is deprecated on FreeBSD

I'd rather not remove CDDA support completely, that one works, and can be useful.

Since CDDA has an hard dependency on CDDB, when and if CDDB is removed from the tree, at that point I will have no option than remove CDDA support, uynless patches come up separating CDDB from CDDA.

BTW porter's handbook requires deprecation time before removing a port, if you really want to remove libcddb from the ports tree in a month, you should set an expiration day ASAP. While there is no rule, since the port is not broken [2] and this is not security related you should really allow two months deprecation. But if you're in an hurry I have no objection with a shorter timeframe.


[1] https://github.com/audacious-media-player/audacious-plugins/blob/master/src/cdaudio/cdaudio-ng.cc  as you can see CDDB library calls are embedded with no define protection.

[2] the library itself compiles, can be linked against and works, there are at least two working server implementations one can use it against, one of which not requiring registration, so it cannot be said to really be broken.
